subject: Cloud Computing
title: The XaaS Explosion
description: A micro-lesson that makes The XaaS Explosion usable.

hook.text: IaaS, PaaS, SaaS — those were just the start. Now everything is 'as a Service.' Literally everything.

buildup.text: DBaaS is database as a service. AIaaS is artificial intelligence. Even DRaaS — disaster recovery.

discovery.text: The pattern is always the same: take something complex, manage it, and sell access through an API.

twist.text: The XaaS trend means fewer companies build core infrastructure. Most just consume other people's APIs.

climax.text: Modern apps are assembled from dozens of services. The 'X' in XaaS is whatever you can imagine.

punchline.text: If it exists, someone sells it as a service now.

quiz.question: What does the 'XaaS' trend represent?
options: Any technology or capability packaged and sold as a managed service
A specific cloud provider's premium service tier
Experimental cloud services still in beta testing
